page: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz and includes 38 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 7340 GB of local storage, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by poor ratings in Geekbench single-core tests and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. However, the server demonstrates robust multi-core and memory subsystem performance. Multi-core CPU benchmarks, including stress-ng and PassMark, fall into the average tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is strong. Notably, uncached memory bandwidth for both large-block reads and writes is highly efficient, ranking in the top 25% of tested hardware. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput consistently perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M64 represents a high-density resource profile tailored for enterprise workloads where memory capacity and local storage volume are the primary constraints rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed. The tradeoff of lower single-core performance is offset by the massive 1 TB memory footprint and 7.3 TB of local storage. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers, big data analytics, and enterprise resource planning applications that demand substantial memory capacity and high multi-threaded throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 1024.0 GB of RAM and 7340 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core performance but strong uncached memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. Multi-core processing, database operations, and Redis throughput perform at average levels. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core and substantial local storage, the M64 is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large relational databases, and in-memory analytics where massive memory capacity is prioritized over single-threaded compute speed.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and 7340 GB of local storage, designed for large databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64 is an Intel-powered server with 64 dedicated vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 7340 GB local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring one terabyte of RAM and substantial local storage for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1024 GB of RAM
Strong uncached memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression
Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory enterprise applications
Runs on Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor with x86_64 architecture
